msgs.noSensorForRae: No sensor selected for RAE output.

errorMsgs.invalidInput: Invalid input for {field}: "{value}" is not a number.
errorMsgs.invalidNumber: All inputs must be valid numbers.
errorMsgs.badEccentricity: Eccentricity must be between 0 and 1 (exclusive) for a closed orbit.
errorMsgs.badSemiMajorAxis: Semi-major axis must be greater than 0.
errorMsgs.noSensorForRae: RAE conversion requires a sensor to be selected.
errorMsgs.noRaeData: No RAE data available. Run a conversion first with a sensor selected.
errorMsgs.notASatellite: Selected object is not a satellite.
errorMsgs.couldNotPropagate: Could not propagate satellite to current time.

help.overview: The Reference Frame Transforms calculator converts a position (and optionally velocity) between every coordinate frame KeepTrack understands: J2000 (ECI), ITRF (ECEF), TEME, Geodetic (LLA), RAE (Topocentric), RA/Dec (Geocentric), and Classical Elements. Type a state in any one frame and read it out in all the others at once.
help.imgAlt: The Reference Frame Transforms menu with input fields and converted outputs
help.imgCaption: Enter a state in one frame; every other frame is computed in one click.
help.frames:
• J2000 (ECI), TEME: Earth centered inertial frames; TEME is the frame TLEs are published in.
  • ITRF (ECEF): Earth centered, Earth fixed; rotates with the planet.
  • Geodetic (LLA): latitude, longitude, and altitude.
  • RAE (Topocentric): range, azimuth, and elevation as seen from the selected sensor.
  • RA/Dec (Geocentric): right ascension and declination.
  • Classical Elements: Keplerian orbital elements, with period, apogee, and perigee in the output.
help.howToUse:
1. Open the menu from the bottom bar (Tools mode).
  2. Pick an Input Frame; the input fields change to match.
  3. Type the values, or click Load Selected Satellite to fill them from the currently selected satellite.
  4. For Cartesian frames, check Include Velocity to convert the velocity vector too; Classical Elements output needs it.
  5. Click Convert. Every other frame's values appear below, formatted per the Output Format selector.
  6. With a sensor selected and an RAE result computed, Draw Line renders the line from the sensor to that point in the 3D view.
help.tip1: RAE input and output need a sensor selected first; the field shows the sensor in use so you always know the reference site.
help.tip2: The DMS output format presents angles as degrees, minutes, and seconds, which is convenient for telescope pointing.
help.tip3: Classical Elements conversion requires velocity: without it the output reads that it needs velocity rather than guessing an orbit.
